title: New ransomware campaign targeting cloud infrastructure
description: Security researchers have identified a new ransomware variant specifically designed to target cloud-based infrastructure. The campaign uses compromised CI/CD pipelines to deploy malicious code.

ai_analysis.impact_assessment: The ransomware campaign targeting cloud infrastructure poses a significant threat to organizations relying on cloud services. By exploiting compromised CI/CD pipelines, attackers can deploy malicious code at scale, potentially leading to data loss, service outages, and financial damage.
recommended_actions: Immediately review and secure CI/CD pipelines by implementing multi-factor authentication (MFA) and restricting access to critical components.
Conduct a thorough audit of cloud infrastructure for potential vulnerabilities and apply necessary patches or updates.
Implement robust backup and disaster recovery plans, ensuring backups are stored offline and regularly tested for integrity.

title: Show HN: Open-source vulnerability scanner
description: New tool automatically scans dependencies for known vulnerabilities and suggests patches. Gaining traction in the security community.

ai_analysis.impact_assessment: Potential security vulnerability with low severity.

title: A vulnerability was found in Tencent AI-Infra-Guard 4.0. The affected element is an unknown...
description: A vulnerability was found in Tencent AI-Infra-Guard 4.0. The affected element is an unknown function of the file common/websocket/task_manager.go of the component Task Detail Endpoint. Performing a manipulation results in information disclosure. The attack may be initiated remotely. The exploit has been made public and could be used. The vendor was contacted early about this disclosure but did not respond in any way.

ai_analysis.impact_assessment: The vulnerability in Tencent AI-Infra-Guard 4.0 allows for potential information disclosure via the Task Detail Endpoint. This could lead to unauthorized access to sensitive data, impacting confidentiality and potentially enabling further attacks.
recommended_actions: Immediately apply network-level restrictions to limit access to the affected endpoint (common/websocket/task_manager.go).
Monitor for any unusual activity or unauthorized access attempts targeting the Task Detail Endpoint.
Engage with Tencent or community forums for potential patches or mitigation strategies, and prepare for rapid deployment once available.

title: A vulnerability was found in CodeAstro Online Classroom 1.0. This vulnerability affects unknown...
description: A vulnerability was found in CodeAstro Online Classroom 1.0. This vulnerability affects unknown code of the file /OnlineClassroom/addassessment.php of the component Parameter Handler. Performing a manipulation of the argument deleteid results in sql injection. The attack is possible to be carried out remotely. The exploit has been made public and could be used.

ai_analysis.impact_assessment: The SQL injection vulnerability in CodeAstro Online Classroom 1.0 could allow attackers to manipulate database queries, potentially leading to data breaches, unauthorized access, or data loss. Since the exploit is public, the risk of exploitation is significantly increased.
recommended_actions: Immediately apply input validation and parameterized queries to sanitize user inputs in the affected component (/OnlineClassroom/addassessment.php).
Patch or update the application to a secure version if a fix is available from the vendor.
Monitor logs for suspicious activity, especially related to the 'deleteid' parameter, and implement Web Application Firewall (WAF) rules to block malicious requests.

title: CVE-2026-2602 - The Twentig plugin for WordPress is vulnerable to Stored Cross-Site Scripting via the 'featuredImage
description: The Twentig plugin for WordPress is vulnerable to Stored Cross-Site Scripting via the 'featuredImageSizeWidth' parameter in versions up to, and including, 1.9.7 due to insufficient input sanitization and output escaping. This makes it possible for authenticated attackers, with Contributor-level access and above, to inject arbitrary web scripts in pages that will execute whenever a user accesses an injected page.

title: CVE-2026-4851 - GRID::Machine versions through 0.127 for Perl allows arbitrary code execution via unsafe deserializa
description:
GRID::Machine versions through 0.127 for Perl allows arbitrary code execution via unsafe deserialization.

GRID::Machine provides Remote Procedure Calls (RPC) over SSH for Perl. The client connects to remote hosts to execute code on them. A compromised or malicious remote host can execute arbitrary code back on the client through unsafe deserialization in the RPC protocol.

read_operation() in lib/GRID/Machine/Message.pm deserialises values from the remote side using eval()

  $arg .= '$VAR1';
  my $val = eval "no strict; $arg"; # line 40-41

$arg is raw bytes from the protocol pipe. A compromised remote host can embed arbitrary perl in the Dumper-formatted response:

  $VAR1 = do { system("..."); };

This executes on the client silently on every RPC call, as the return values remain correct.

This functionality is by design but the trust requirement for the remote host is not documented in the distribution.

ai_analysis.impact_assessment: Potential security vulnerability with critical severity. CVE: CVE-2026-4851.
